justification: All pass criteria met: 8 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria covering happy path (AC-1, AC-2), already-shipped/in-transit rejection (AC-3, AC-4), already-cancelled idempotency (AC-5), authorization (AC-6), plus concurrency (AC-8). 4 explicit escalations surfacing ambiguities: cancellation window states, refund policy, partial cancellation, and who can cancel. confidence.level=45 well under 65 threshold. edge_cases section populated with 6 scenarios. Business rules are clearly flagged as assumptions rather than presented as facts.

observations.text: 8 acceptance criteria far exceed the minimum 3, covering happy path, terminal state rejections, authorization, idempotency, and concurrency
All 4 escalations are typed as 'ambiguity' with options and recommendations — not presented as decided facts
confidence.level=45 correctly reflects the underspecified input
low_confidence_areas explicitly lists cancellation window cutoff, refund policy, partial cancellation, time-based window, and admin/support permissions
edge_cases section is thorough with 6 scenarios including concurrency, sub-second cancellation, network failure, and partial cancellation

Defines 'Cancellation Fee' and 'Cancellation Window' in ubiquitous_language and references cancellation fees in AC-2 and BR-4 — while flagged as assumptions in escalations, embedding them in acceptance criteria blurs the line between flagged assumption and presented fact
The recommendation in each escalation is clearly labeled as a recommendation with alternatives, not an assumed decision

agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Order cancellation is a core lifecycle operation in logistics and courier systems. A user-initiated cancellation must respect the physical realities of fulfillment — once a package is in transit or delivered, cancellation becomes increasingly complex or impossible. The PM statement 'Users should be able to cancel their order' is underspecified across multiple critical dimensions: which order states allow cancellation, refund policy, partial cancellation, a
